_meta.notes: Subverted-default race. The genre says elves are 'long-lived, ethereal, wise mystics'. Here, elves live a perfectly normal 60-70 years, are intensely commercial, and run banking + information brokerages. The 'serene mystic' mannerism is a cultivated commercial act they drop entirely once they trust you. Some of the rudest people in the world if you make it past their second account review.

physicalNotes: Average height 5'10". Slender. The pointed ears are real but smaller than legend has it. Most adults have ink under their fingernails from contracts.

racialFeatures.label: The Act
racialFeatures.description: All elves are taught from age six to perform serene-mystic etherealness in front of strangers. The performance is a commercial advantage — it makes other races offer better terms. They drop it at home, in their counting houses, and around anyone they're tired of.

racialFeatures.label: Ledger-mind
racialFeatures.description: Elves track favors, debts, and obligations across every social interaction with mechanical precision. They will remind you of a coffee they bought you in 2014. They are not joking.

racialFeatures.label: Salt-tongue
racialFeatures.description: When the act drops, elven speech becomes terse, profane, and surgical. Their rudeness, when it lands, is precise enough to be admired.

subPopulations.label: Branchmen
subPopulations.summary: The banking families. Each Branchman house has a single specialty (grain futures, marine insurance, foreign letters of credit). They will not tell you which.

subPopulations.label: Clearseers
subPopulations.summary: Information brokers. They sell what is going to happen, sourced from a network of paid villagers across all five spokes. The act is at its thickest with them.

subPopulations.label: Salt-folk
subPopulations.summary: Wholesale traders. Bulk salt, bulk grain, bulk timber. Loud, profane, and direct — the only elves who never bother with the act.

subPopulations.label: Vellum
subPopulations.summary: Contract scribes. They write the actual paper. Considered the most dangerous elves because nobody reads what a Vellum has written carefully enough.

voice: the act is breathy and rhythmic; off-act is clipped, profane, and full of numbers.
